    19→import sqlite3
    20→import sys
    21→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    22→from pathlib import Path
    23→
    24→from _transcript_discover import (
    25→    default_global_transcript_root,
    26→    discover_source_files,
    27→    infer_project_slug,
    28→    transcript_matches_repo,
    29→)
    30→from _transcript_import import import_transcript, transcript_has_content
    31→from _transcript_parse import parse_transcript, slugify
    32→
    33→
    34→def main() -> int:
    35→    args = parse_args()
    36→    root = Path(args.root).resolve()
    37→    if not root.exists():
    38→        raise SystemExit(f"root does not exist: {root}")
    39→
    40→    repo_slug = slugify(args.project_name.strip() or root.name, fallback="project")
    41→    global_root = resolve_global_root(args.global_root)
    42→    activation_time = args.activation_time or _now_iso()
    43→
    44→    # Step 1: On-demand sync — discover and import for current project only
    45→    imported = _sync_current_project(root, repo_slug, global_root)
    46→
    47→    # Step 2: Query global index for latest session
    48→    session = _find_latest_session(global_root, root, repo_slug, activation_time)
    49→
    50→    if session is None:
    51→        payload = {
    52→            "found": False,
    53→            "project": repo_slug,
    54→            "repo": root.as_posix(),
    55→            "imported_count": imported,
    56→            "message": "No previous session found for this project.",
    57→        }
    58→    else:
    59→        clean_path = session.get("global_clean_path", "")
    60→        clean_content = ""
    61→        if clean_path and Path(clean_path).exists():
    62→            try:
    63→                clean_content = Path(clean_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    64→            except OSError:
    65→                pass
    66→
    67→        payload = {
    68→            "found": True,
    69→            "project": repo_slug,
    70→            "repo": root.as_posix(),
    71→            "imported_count": imported,
    72→            "client": session.get("client", ""),
    73→            "session_id": session.get("session_id", ""),
    74→            "title": session.get("title", ""),
    75→            "started_at": session.get("started_at", ""),
    76→            "cwd": session.get("cwd", ""),
    77→            "branch": session.get("branch", ""),
    78→            "message_count": session.get("message_count", 0),
    79→            "tool_event_count": session.get("tool_event_count", 0),
    80→            "global_clean_path": clean_path,
    81→            "clean_content": clean_content,
    82→        }
    83→
    84→    if args.format == "json":
    85→        print(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False))
    86→    else:
    87→        print(format_text(payload))
    88→    return 0
    89→
    90→
    91→def _sync_current_project(root: Path, repo_slug: str, global_root: Path) -> int:
    92→    """Discover and import transcripts for the current project only."""
    93→    discovered = discover_source_files()
    94→    imported = 0
    95→
    96→    for client, source in discovered:
    97→        try:
    98→            parsed = parse_transcript(client, source)
    99→        except Exception:
   100→            continue
   101→
   102→        if not transcript_has_content(parsed):
   103→            continue
   104→
   105→        if not transcript_matches_repo(parsed, root, repo_slug):
   106→            continue
   107→
   108→        try:
   109→            import_transcript(
   110→                parsed=parsed,
   111→                root=root,
   112→                global_root=global_root,
   113→                project_slug=repo_slug,
   114→                raw_upload_permission="not-set",
   115→                mirror_to_repo=True,
   116→            )
   117→            imported += 1
   118→        except Exception:
   119→            continue
   120→
   121→    return imported
   122→
   123→
   124→def _find_latest_session(
   125→    global_root: Path,
   126→    root: Path,
   127→    repo_slug: str,
   128→    activation_time: str,
   129→) -> dict | None:
   130→    """Query the SQLite index for the latest session before activation_time."""
   131→    db_path = global_root / "index" / "search.sqlite"
   132→    if not db_path.exists():
   133→        # Fallback: try sessions.jsonl
   134→        return _find_latest_from_jsonl(global_root, root, repo_slug, activation_time)
   135→
   136→    try:
   137→        conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
   138→        try:
   139→            conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
   140→            cursor = conn.execute(
   141→                """
   142→                SELECT * FROM transcripts
   143→                WHERE started_at < ?
   144→                ORDER BY started_at DESC
   145→                LIMIT 20
   146→                """,
   147→                (activation_time,),
   148→            )
   149→            rows = cursor.fetchall()
   150→        finally:
   151→            conn.close()
   152→    except (sqlite3.Error, OSError):
   153→        return _find_latest_from_jsonl(global_root, root, repo_slug, activation_time)
   154→
   155→    resolved_root = root.resolve().as_posix()
   156→    for row in rows:
   157→        row_dict = dict(row)
   158→        cwd = row_dict.get("cwd", "")
   159→        project = row_dict.get("project", "")
   160→        # Match by cwd path or project slug
   161→        if _cwd_matches(cwd, resolved_root) or project == repo_slug:
   162→            return row_dict
   163→
   164→    return None
   165→
   166→
   167→def _find_latest_from_jsonl(
   168→    global_root: Path,
   169→    root: Path,
   170→    repo_slug: str,
   171→    activation_time: str,
   172→) -> dict | None:
   173→    """Fallback: scan sessions.jsonl when SQLite index doesn't exist."""
   174→    jsonl_path = global_root / "index" / "sessions.jsonl"
   175→    if not jsonl_path.exists():
   176→        return None
   177→
   178→    resolved_root = root.resolve().as_posix()
   179→    candidates: list[dict] = []
   180→
   181→    try:
   182→        with jsonl_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
   183→            for line in fh:
   184→                line = line.strip()
   185→                if not line:
   186→                    continue
   187→                try:
   188→                    entry = json.loads(line)
   189→                except json.JSONDecodeError:
   190→                    continue
   191→
   192→                started = entry.get("started_at", "")
   193→                if started >= activation_time:
   194→                    continue
   195→
   196→                cwd = entry.get("cwd", "")
   197→                project = entry.get("project", "")
   198→                if _cwd_matches(cwd, resolved_root) or project == repo_slug:
   199→                    candidates.append(entry)
   200→    except OSError:
   201→        return None
   202→
   203→    if not candidates:
   204→        return None
   205→
   206→    # Sort by started_at descending, return latest
   207→    candidates.sort(key=lambda e: e.get("started_at", ""), reverse=True)
   208→    return candidates[0]
   209→
   210→
   211→def _cwd_matches(cwd: str, resolved_root: str) -> bool:
   212→    """Check if a transcript's cwd matches the repo root (exact or subdirectory)."""
   213→    if not cwd:
   214→        return False
   215→    try:
   216→        cwd_resolved = Path(cwd).resolve().as_posix()
   217→        if cwd_resolved == resolved_root:
   218→            return True
   219→        # Check if cwd is a subdirectory of resolved_root
   220→        return cwd_resolved.startswith(resolved_root + "/")
   221→    except OSError:
   222→        return False
   223→
   224→
   225→def _now_iso() -> str:
   226→    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   227→
   228→
   229→def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
   230→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   231→        description="On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall for MemoryTree.",
   232→    )
   233→    parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Target repository root.")
   234→    parser.add_argument(
   235→        "--project-name",
   236→        default="",
   237→        help="Project label. Defaults to the repo folder name.",
   238→    )
   239→    parser.add_argument(
   240→        "--global-root",
   241→        default="",
   242→        help="Override the global transcript root. Defaults to ~/.memorytree/transcripts.",
   243→    )
   244→    parser.add_argument(
   245→        "--activation-time",
   246→        default="",
   247→        help="ISO timestamp of session activation. Transcripts started at or after this time are excluded.",
   248→    )
   249→    parser.add_argument(
   250→        "--format",
   251→        choices=("text", "json"),
   252→        default="text",
   253→        help="Output format.",
   254→    )
   255→    return parser.parse_args()
   256→
   257→
   258→def resolve_global_root(path_value: str) -> Path:
   259→    if path_value:
   260→        return Path(path_value).expanduser().resolve()
   261→    return default_global_transcript_root()
   262→
   263→
   264→def format_text(payload: dict) -> str:
   265→    if not payload.get("found"):
   266→        return (
   267→            f"project: {payload.get('project', '?')}\n"
   268→            f"imported: {payload.get('imported_count', 0)}\n"
   269→            f"result: {payload.get('message', 'No previous session found.')}"
   270→        )
   271→
   272→    lines = [
   273→        f"project: {payload.get('project', '')}",
   274→        f"client: {payload.get('client', '')}",
   275→        f"session_id: {payload.get('session_id', '')}",
   276→        f"title: {payload.get('title', '')}",
   277→        f"started_at: {payload.get('started_at', '')}",
   278→        f"cwd: {payload.get('cwd', '')}",
   279→        f"branch: {payload.get('branch', '')}",
   280→        f"messages: {payload.get('message_count', 0)}",
   281→        f"tool_events: {payload.get('tool_event_count', 0)}",
   282→        f"imported_this_sync: {payload.get('imported_count', 0)}",
   283→        f"clean_transcript: {payload.get('global_clean_path', '')}",
   284→    ]
   285→
   286→    content = payload.get("clean_content", "")
   287→    if content:
   288→        lines.append("")
   289→        lines.append("--- clean transcript content ---")
   290→        lines.append(content)
   291→
   292→    return "\n".join(lines)
   293→
   294→
   295→if __name__ == "__main__":
   296→    sys.exit(main())

```python
LOGGER_NAME = "memorytree"

def setup_logging(log_level: str = "info") -> logging.Logger:
    logger = logging.getLogger(LOGGER_NAME)
    if logger.handlers:
        return logger
    level = _resolve_level(log_level)
    logger.setLevel(level)
    formatter = logging.Formatter("%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s", datefmt="%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
    stderr_handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stderr)
    stderr_handler.setLevel(level)
    stderr_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
    logger.addHandler(stderr_handler)
    log_file = _log_file_path()
    log_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    try:
        file_handler = logging.FileHandler(log_file, encoding="utf-8")
        file_handler.setLevel(level)
        file_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
        logger.addHandler(file_handler)
    except OSError:
        logger.warning("Could not open log file: %s", log_file)
    return logger

def get_logger() -> logging.Logger:
    return logging.getLogger(LOGGER_NAME)

def _log_file_path() -> Path:
    today = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    return Path.home() / ".memorytree" / "logs" / f"heartbeat-{today}.log"

def _resolve_level(level_str: str) -> int:
    mapping = {"debug": logging.DEBUG, "info": logging.INFO, "warn": logging.WARNING, "warning": logging.WARNING, "error": logging.ERROR}
    return mapping.get(level_str.lower(), logging.INFO)
```

  - Has methods: `debug(msg)`, `info(msg)`, `warn(msg)`, `error(msg)`, `exception(msg, error?)`
  - Writes to stderr AND appends to a log file
  - Log file path: `~/.memorytree/logs/heartbeat-YYYY-MM-DD.log`
  - Format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS [LEVEL] message`

Port the following Python code:

```python
LOGGER_NAME = "memorytree"

def setup_logging(log_level: str = "info") -> logging.Logger:
    logger = logging.getLogger(LOGGER_NAME)
    if logger.handlers:
        return logger
    level = _resolve_level(log_level)
    logger.setLevel(level)
    formatter = logging.Formatter("%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s", datefmt="%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
    stderr_handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stderr)
    stderr_handler.setLevel(level)
    stderr_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
    logger.addHandler(stderr_handler)
    log_file = _log_file_path()
    log_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    try:
        file_handler = logging.FileHandler(log_file, encoding="utf-8")
        file_handler.setLevel(level)
        file_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
        logger.addHandler(file_handler)
    except OSError:
        logger.warning("Could not open log file: %s", log_file)
    return logger

def get_logger() -> logging.Logger:
    return logging.getLogger(LOGGER_NAME)

def _log_file_path() -> Path:
    today = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    return Path.home() / ".memorytree" / "logs" / f"heartbeat-{today}.log"

def _resolve_level(level_str: str) -> int:
    mapping = {"debug": logging.DEBUG, "info": logging.INFO, "warn": logging.WARNING, "warning": logging.WARNING, "error": logging.ERROR}
    return mapping.get(level_str.lower(), logging.INFO)
```

TypeScript implementation:

```typescript
export type LogLevel = 'debug' | 'info' | 'warn' | 'error'

export interface Logger {
  debug(msg: string): void
  info(msg: string): void
  warn(msg: string): void
  error(msg: string): void
  exception(msg: string, err?: unknown): void
}
```

- Create a `MemoryTreeLogger` class that implements Logger
- Store the log level as a numeric priority (debug=0, info=1, warn=2, error=3)
- Each method checks if the message level >= configured level before writing
- Write to stderr using `process.stderr.write()`
- Write to file using `appendFileSync()` (Windows-safe for concurrent access)
- `exception()` is like `error()` but also appends the error stack if available
- `setupLogging(logLevel)` creates a singleton logger
- `getLogger()` returns the singleton (throws if not setup yet — or auto-creates with defaults)
- Export: `setupLogging`, `getLogger`, `Logger` type, `LogLevel` type

input.prompt:
Write test files for the Phase 2 heartbeat modules in D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow. Create these test files:

1. `tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/config.ts
2. `tests/heartbeat/lock.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/lock.ts  
3. `tests/heartbeat/alert.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/alert.ts
4. `tests/heartbeat/log.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/log.ts
5. `tests/heartbeat/heartbeat.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts

CONSTRAINTS:
- ESM project, vitest globals enabled, import from 'vitest'
- Use `mkdtempSync` for temp dirs, clean up in afterEach with `rmSync`
- For modules that use `homedir()` or fixed paths, use `vi.mock('node:os')` or `vi.spyOn` to redirect to temp dirs
- Import using `.js` extensions: `from '../../src/heartbeat/config.js'`

### config.test.ts (~12 tests):
Read the source first: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\config.ts`
- loadConfig: default when no file exists, valid TOML, invalid TOML, missing fields use defaults
- saveConfig: roundtrip (save then load matches), projects serialization
- intervalToSeconds: '5m'→300, '1h'→3600, '30s'→30, invalid→300(default)
- registerProject: adds new project, skips duplicate (by resolved path)
- configPath/memorytreeRoot: return correct paths

Since these modules use `homedir()` for paths, the tests need to mock the config path. The simplest approach: 
- Mock the `configPath()` function to return a path in a temp dir
- OR use `vi.mock('node:os')` to override `homedir()`

Actually, the cleanest approach for config.ts: just test intervalToSeconds and registerProject directly (they don't use filesystem). For loadConfig/saveConfig, mock `configPath()` to return a temp path.

### lock.test.ts (~8 tests):
Read the source first: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\lock.ts`
- acquireLock/releaseLock: acquire succeeds, release removes, acquire fails when held by current process
- readLockPid: returns PID when locked, null when not locked
- isProcessAlive: current PID is alive, PID 0 is not alive, very large PID is not alive
- Stale detection: lock file with dead PID gets reclaimed

### alert.test.ts (~10 tests):
Read the source first: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\alert.ts`
- writeAlert: creates alert, deduplicates (updates count), caps at MAX_ALERTS
- writeAlertWithThreshold: doesn't write until threshold reached
- resetFailureCount: resets counter
- readAlerts: empty when no file, returns array
- clearAlerts: removes file
- formatAlertsForDisplay: formats correctly, handles empty

### log.test.ts (~5 tests):
Read the source first: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\log.ts`
- setupLogging: returns Logger, writes to file
- getLogger: returns same instance
- Log levels: debug filtered when level is info
- Log file path includes date

### heartbeat.test.ts (~8 tests):
Read the source first: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\heartbeat.ts`
- scanSensitive: detects API keys, passwords, tokens, bearer tokens; no false positives on normal text
- SENSITIVE_PATTERNS: test each regex pattern individually
- The main/runHeartbeat/processProject functions need heavy mocking of filesystem and git - keep tests focused on scanSensitive and the patterns

For the filesystem-dependent tests, the best approach is to:
1. Create a temp dir
2. Override the module's path functions using vi.mock or vi.spyOn
3. Write test files there
4. Clean up

WRITE all 5 test files using the Write tool. Read the source files first to understand exact function signatures.

<result>Zero diagnostics. The implementation is complete.

---

**Summary of `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\lock.ts`:**

The file is a faithful TypeScript port of `scripts/_lock_utils.py` with five public exports:

| Export | Description |
|---|---|
| `lockPath()` | Returns `~/.memorytree/heartbeat.lock` |
| `acquireLock()` | Acquires the PID lock with stale/corrupt detection; uses `O_CREAT | O_EXCL` for race safety |
| `releaseLock()` | Removes the lock file |
| `readLockPid()` | Reads the stored PID or returns `null` |
| `isProcessAlive(pid)` | Cross-platform liveness check (Unix: `process.kill(pid, 0)` with EPERM handling; Windows: `tasklist /FI "PID eq ..."`) |

Key implementation choices:
- Uses `node:fs` constants (`O_CREAT | O_EXCL | O_WRONLY`) for atomic lock creation, matching the Python `os.open` approach.
- On Unix, `process.kill(pid, 0)` mirrors Python's `os.kill(pid, 0)`, with `EPERM` treated as "alive" (same as Python's `PermissionError` branch).
- On Windows, `execFileSync('tasklist', ...)` replaces the Python `ctypes.windll.kernel32.OpenProcess` approach, as noted in the requirements.
- All imports use `.js`-free paths since they only import from `node:` built-ins (no local module imports needed).
- Compiles cleanly under `strict: true`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true` with zero errors and zero VS Code diagnostics.</result>

<summary>Agent "Implement alert.ts" completed</summary>
<result>No errors in `alert.ts`. The file compiles cleanly under `strict: true`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true`, and `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`.

Here is a summary of what was implemented in `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\alert.ts`:

**Exported constants:**
- `MAX_ALERTS` (100) -- cap on stored alerts
- `ALERT_TYPES` -- `ReadonlySet<string>` with `no_remote`, `sensitive_match`, `push_failed`, `lock_held`
- `FAILURE_THRESHOLD` (3) -- consecutive failures before an alert is persisted

**Exported type:**
- `Alert` interface with `timestamp`, `project`, `type`, `message`, `count`, plus index signature for flexibility

**Exported functions (faithful port of each Python counterpart):**
- `alertsPath()` -- resolves `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`
- `readAlerts()` -- reads and parses the alerts file, returning `[]` on missing/corrupt data
- `writeAlert(project, alertType, message)` -- appends or dedup-updates an alert, trims to `MAX_ALERTS`
- `writeAlertWithThreshold(project, alertType, message)` -- increments failure count; only writes alert when threshold is reached
- `resetFailureCount(project, alertType)` -- removes a failure counter (immutable object rebuild)
- `clearAlerts()` -- deletes the alerts file
- `formatAlertsForDisplay(alerts)` -- renders alerts as indented human-readable lines

**Design decisions matching project conventions:**
- Self-contained UTC timestamp helper (`utcTimestamp()`) to avoid cross-module dependency
- Immutable patterns throughout (spread operator for object updates, `readonly` arrays)
- No `.js` extension imports needed since the file has no local imports
- `node:fs`, `node:os`, `node:path` used consistently with the existing `config.ts` style</result>

<summary>Agent "Implement log.ts" completed</summary>
<result>Zero diagnostics for `log.ts`. The file compiles cleanly under the project's strict settings.

Here is a summary of what was implemented in `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\log.ts`:

**Exported types:**
- `LogLevel` -- union type `'debug' | 'info' | 'warn' | 'error'`
- `Logger` -- interface with `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`, and `exception` methods

**Exported functions:**
- `setupLogging(logLevel?)` -- creates a singleton `MemoryTreeLogger`; only the first call's `logLevel` takes effect (mirrors Python's "if logger.handlers: return" guard)
- `getLogger()` -- returns the singleton, auto-creating with `'info'` level if not yet set up (convenient default rather than throwing)
- `_resetLogger()` -- resets the singleton for test isolation

**MemoryTreeLogger class (internal):**
- Stores level as numeric priority (debug=0, info=1, warn=2, error=3); each log method checks `LEVEL_PRIORITY[level] < this.levelPriority` before writing
- Writes to `process.stderr.write()` and `appendFileSync()` -- both Windows-safe for concurrent access
- Log file path: `~/.memorytree/logs/heartbeat-YYYY-MM-DD.log` (UTC date, matching Python)
- Format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS [LEVEL] message` (UTC timestamps, matching Python's `datefmt`)
- `exception()` appends `err.stack` if the error is an `Error` instance, otherwise `String(err)`
- If the log directory cannot be created, falls back to stderr-only mode with a warning (mirrors Python's `except OSError`)

**Helpers:**
- `resolveLevel()` -- maps strings to `LogLevel`, including `'warning'` -> `'warn'` alias, defaulting to `'info'`
- `logFilePathForToday()` -- builds the date-stamped log file path
- `timestamp()` -- formats UTC time as `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS`

The two pre-existing errors in `heartbeat.ts` (lines 40 and 77) are unrelated to this change. One of them (`Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'LogLevel | undefined'`) is actually caused by `heartbeat.ts` passing an untyped `string` to the now-properly-typed `setupLogging` -- that file will need a minor cast or type narrowing when it is ported.</result>

<summary>Agent "Write heartbeat module tests" completed</summary>
<result>All 69 tests across all 5 test files pass. Here is a summary of what was created:

**Test files created (69 tests total, all passing):**

1. **`D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\config.test.ts`** (18 tests)
   - `configPath / memorytreeRoot` (2): correct path structure
   - `loadConfig` (4): defaults when no file, valid TOML parsing, invalid TOML fallback, missing fields use defaults
   - `saveConfig` (2): roundtrip save/load, empty projects serialization
   - `intervalToSeconds` (7): 5m->300, 1h->3600, 30s->30, invalid->300 default, 0m->300, whitespace handling, case-insensitivity
   - `registerProject` (3): adds new project, skips duplicate, does not mutate original config

2. **`D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\lock.test.ts`** (14 tests)
   - `lockPath` (1): correct path
   - `acquireLock / releaseLock` (4): succeed when free, fail when held, release removes file, re-acquire after release
   - `readLockPid` (3): returns PID when locked, null when no file, null for corrupt
   - `isProcessAlive` (4): current PID alive, PID 0 not alive, negative PID not alive, large PID not alive
   - `stale lock detection` (2): reclaims dead PID lock, reclaims corrupt lock

3. **`D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\alert.test.ts`** (17 tests)
   - `alertsPath` (1): correct path
   - `readAlerts` (1): empty when no file
   - `writeAlert` (5): creates alert, deduplicates with count update, different types not merged, different projects not merged, caps at MAX_ALERTS
   - `writeAlertWithThreshold` (2): no write below threshold, writes after threshold
   - `resetFailureCount` (2): resets counter, no-op for nonexistent key
   - `clearAlerts` (2): removes all alerts, no-op when no file
   - `formatAlertsForDisplay` (4): empty string for empty array, single alert formatting, count suffix for count>1, multiple alerts on separate lines

4. **`D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\log.test.ts`** (7 tests)
   - `setupLogging` (2): returns Logger with all methods, writes to file
   - `getLogger` (2): returns same singleton, auto-creates if not set up
   - `log level filtering` (2): debug filtered at info level, debug visible at debug level
   - `log file naming` (1): file name includes YYYY-MM-DD date

5. **`D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\heartbeat.test.ts`** (13 tests)
   - `scanSensitive` (8): detects API keys, passwords, tokens, known prefixes (sk-, ghp_), Bearer tokens, no false positives on normal text, stops after first match
   - `SENSITIVE_PATTERNS individual patterns` (5): api_key variants, password/passwd/pwd, secret/token, known prefixes (sk-, pk_live_, sk_live_, ghp_, gho_, glpat-), Bearer token length requirements

**Approach used:**
- `vi.mock('node:os')` to override `homedir()` and redirect all filesystem operations (config, lock, alerts, logs) to `mkdtempSync` temp directories
- Each test file creates a fresh temp dir in `beforeEach` and cleans up with `rmSync` in `afterEach`
- The log tests use `_resetLogger()` to clear the singleton between tests
- ESM imports with `.js` extensions, vitest globals from `'vitest'`</result>

EOF
)" && git push

   223→def resolve_scaffold_paths(root: Path, dt: datetime) -> ScaffoldPaths:
   224→    date_token = dt.strftime("%Y%m%d")
   225→    date_label = dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
   226→    time_label = dt.strftime("%H:%M")
   227→    chat_filename = dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M.md")
   228→
   229→    goal_dir = root / "Memory" / "01_goals"

   231→    chat_dir = root / "Memory" / "03_chat_logs"
   232→

   234→    if goal_version is None:
   235→        goal_version = "001"
   236→    previous_version = f"v{int(goal_version) - 1:03d}" if int(goal_version) > 1 else "none"
   237→
   238→    todo_version = find_latest_todo_version(todo_dir, goal_version)
   239→    if todo_version is None:
   240→        todo_version = "001"
   241→
   242→    goal_path = find_latest_goal_file(goal_dir) or goal_dir / f"goal_v{goal_version}_{date_token}.md"
   243→    todo_path = (

   245→        or todo_dir / f"todo_v{goal_version}_{todo_version}_{date_token}.md"
   246→    )

   248→
   249→    return ScaffoldPaths(

   258→        todo_version=todo_version,
   259→        previous_version=previous_version,
   260→        date_label=date_label,
   261→        time_label=time_label,
   262→    )
   263→

   267→    for rel in DIRS:
   268→        path = root / rel
   269→        if not path.exists():
   270→            created.append(path.relative_to(root).as_posix())
   271→        path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   272→    return created
   273→
   274→
   275→def scaffold_content_files(
   276→    paths: ScaffoldPaths,
   277→    templates: Path,
   278→    goal_summary: str,
   279→    project_name: str,
   280→    force: bool,
   281→) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:
   282→    created: list[str] = []
   283→    preserved: list[str] = []
   284→    pairs = [
   285→        (
   286→            templates / "goal.md",
   287→            paths.goal_path,
   288→            {
   289→                "GOAL_VERSION": paths.goal_version,
   290→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   291→                "PREVIOUS_VERSION": paths.previous_version,
   292→                "GOAL_SUMMARY": goal_summary.strip(),
   293→            },
   294→        ),
   295→        (
   296→            templates / "todo.md",
   297→            paths.todo_path,
   298→            {
   299→                "GOAL_VERSION": paths.goal_version,
   300→                "TODO_SUBVERSION": paths.todo_version,
   301→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   302→            },
   303→        ),
   304→        (
   305→            templates / "chat-log.md",
   306→            paths.chat_path,
   307→            {
   308→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   309→                "TIME": paths.time_label,
   310→                "PROJECT_NAME": project_name.strip(),
   311→            },
   312→        ),
   313→    ]
   314→    for template_path, target_path, values in pairs:
   315→        if write_template(template_path, target_path, force, values):
   316→            created.append(target_path.relative_to(paths.root).as_posix())
   317→        else:
   318→            preserved.append(target_path.relative_to(paths.root).as_posix())
   319→    return created, preserved

   203→    target_path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
   204→    return True
   205→
   206→
   207→@dataclass
   208→class ScaffoldPaths:
   209→    root: Path
   210→    goal_dir: Path
   211→    todo_dir: Path
   212→    chat_dir: Path
   213→    goal_path: Path
   214→    todo_path: Path
   215→    chat_path: Path
   216→    goal_version: str
   217→    todo_version: str
   218→    previous_version: str
   219→    date_label: str
   220→    time_label: str
   221→
   222→
   223→def resolve_scaffold_paths(root: Path, dt: datetime) -> ScaffoldPaths:
   224→    date_token = dt.strftime("%Y%m%d")
   225→    date_label = dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
   226→    time_label = dt.strftime("%H:%M")
   227→    chat_filename = dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M.md")
   228→
   229→    goal_dir = root / "Memory" / "01_goals"
   230→    todo_dir = root / "Memory" / "02_todos"
   231→    chat_dir = root / "Memory" / "03_chat_logs"
   232→
   233→    goal_version = find_latest_version(goal_dir, r"goal_v(\d{3})_\d{8}\.md")
   234→    if goal_version is None:
   235→        goal_version = "001"
   236→    previous_version = f"v{int(goal_version) - 1:03d}" if int(goal_version) > 1 else "none"
   237→
   238→    todo_version = find_latest_todo_version(todo_dir, goal_version)
   239→    if todo_version is None:
   240→        todo_version = "001"
   241→
   242→    goal_path = find_latest_goal_file(goal_dir) or goal_dir / f"goal_v{goal_version}_{date_token}.md"
   243→    todo_path = (
   244→        find_latest_todo_file(todo_dir, goal_version)
   245→        or todo_dir / f"todo_v{goal_version}_{todo_version}_{date_token}.md"
   246→    )
   247→    chat_path = find_latest_chat_log_file(chat_dir) or chat_dir / chat_filename
   248→
   249→    return ScaffoldPaths(
   250→        root=root,
   251→        goal_dir=goal_dir,
   252→        todo_dir=todo_dir,
   253→        chat_dir=chat_dir,
   254→        goal_path=goal_path,
   255→        todo_path=todo_path,
   256→        chat_path=chat_path,
   257→        goal_version=goal_version,
   258→        todo_version=todo_version,
   259→        previous_version=previous_version,
   260→        date_label=date_label,
   261→        time_label=time_label,
   262→    )
   263→
   264→
   265→def create_memory_dirs(root: Path) -> list[str]:
   266→    created: list[str] = []
   267→    for rel in DIRS:
   268→        path = root / rel
   269→        if not path.exists():
   270→            created.append(path.relative_to(root).as_posix())
   271→        path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   272→    return created
   273→
   274→
   275→def scaffold_content_files(
   276→    paths: ScaffoldPaths,
   277→    templates: Path,
   278→    goal_summary: str,
   279→    project_name: str,
   280→    force: bool,
   281→) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:
   282→    created: list[str] = []
   283→    preserved: list[str] = []
   284→    pairs = [
   285→        (
   286→            templates / "goal.md",
   287→            paths.goal_path,
   288→            {
   289→                "GOAL_VERSION": paths.goal_version,
   290→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   291→                "PREVIOUS_VERSION": paths.previous_version,
   292→                "GOAL_SUMMARY": goal_summary.strip(),
   293→            },
   294→        ),
   295→        (
   296→            templates / "todo.md",
   297→            paths.todo_path,
   298→            {
   299→                "GOAL_VERSION": paths.goal_version,
   300→                "TODO_SUBVERSION": paths.todo_version,
   301→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   302→            },
   303→        ),
   304→        (
   305→            templates / "chat-log.md",
   306→            paths.chat_path,
   307→            {
   308→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   309→                "TIME": paths.time_label,
   310→                "PROJECT_NAME": project_name.strip(),
   311→            },
   312→        ),
   313→    ]
   314→    for template_path, target_path, values in pairs:
   315→        if write_template(template_path, target_path, force, values):
   316→            created.append(target_path.relative_to(paths.root).as_posix())
   317→        else:
   318→            preserved.append(target_path.relative_to(paths.root).as_posix())
   319→    return created, preserved
   320→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree without overwriting existing repo policy files.
     4→"""
     5→
     6→from __future__ import annotations
     7→
     8→import argparse
     9→import json
    10→import re
    11→from pathlib import Path
    12→
    13→from _locale_utils import normalize_locale
    14→from _scaffold_utils import (
    15→    build_datetime,
    16→    create_memory_dirs,
    17→    find_external_policy_sources,
    18→    find_latest_chat_log_file,
    19→    find_latest_goal_file,
    20→    find_latest_todo_file,
    21→    resolve_scaffold_paths,
    22→    resolve_template_dir,
    23→    scaffold_content_files,
    24→    write_template,
    25→)
    26→
    27→
    28→def main() -> int:
    29→    args = parse_args()
    30→    root = Path(args.root).resolve()
    31→    if not root.exists():
    32→        raise SystemExit(f"root does not exist: {root}")
    33→
    34→    skill_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    35→    templates = resolve_template_dir(skill_root, root, args.locale)
    36→    effective_locale = normalize_locale(args.locale, root)
    37→    initial_state = detect_state(root)
    38→
    39→    dt = build_datetime(args.date, args.time)
    40→
    41→    created_dirs = create_memory_dirs(root)
    42→    paths = resolve_scaffold_paths(root, dt)
    43→    created_files, preserved_files = scaffold_content_files(
    44→        paths, templates, args.goal_summary, args.project_name, force=False,
    45→    )
    46→
    47→    agents_path = root / "AGENTS.md"
    48→    agents_action = "preserved_existing"
    49→    if not agents_path.exists():
    50→        if write_template(templates / "agents.md", agents_path, False, {}):
    51→            created_files.append("AGENTS.md")
    52→            agents_action = "created"
    53→    else:
    54→        preserved_files.append("AGENTS.md")
    55→    merge_required = agents_action == "preserved_existing" and needs_agents_merge(agents_path)
    56→
    57→    result = {
    58→        "state_before": initial_state,
    59→        "state_after": detect_state(root),
    60→        "requested_locale": args.locale,
    61→        "effective_locale": effective_locale,
    62→        "created_dirs": created_dirs,
    63→        "created_files": created_files,
    64→        "preserved_files": preserved_files,
    65→        "agents_action": agents_action,
    66→        "agents_merge_required": merge_required,
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    if args.format == "json":
    70→        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False))
    71→    else:
    72→        print(format_result_text(result))
    73→    return 0
    74→
    75→
    76→def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    77→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree safely.")
    78→    parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Target repository root.")
    79→    parser.add_argument("--project-name", default="this project", help="Project name used in generated chat logs.")
    80→    parser.add_argument(
    81→        "--goal-summary",
    82→        default="Describe the long-term project goal here.",
    83→        help="Fallback north-star summary when a goal file must be created.",
    84→    )
    85→    parser.add_argument("--locale", default="auto", help="Requested locale: auto, en, or zh-cn.")
    86→    parser.add_argument("--date", default="", help="Override date as YYYY-MM-DD for deterministic runs.")
    87→    parser.add_argument(
    88→        "--time",
    89→        default="",
    90→        help="Override time as HH:MM. If --date is omitted, use the current local date.",
    91→    )
    92→    parser.add_argument("--format", choices=("text", "json"), default="text", help="Output format.")
    93→    return parser.parse_args()
    94→
    95→
    96→def detect_state(root: Path) -> str:
    97→    memory_dir = root / "Memory"
    98→    goal_dir = root / "Memory" / "01_goals"
    99→    todo_dir = root / "Memory" / "02_todos"
   100→    chat_dir = root / "Memory" / "03_chat_logs"
   101→    agents_path = root / "AGENTS.md"
   102→    policy_sources = find_external_policy_sources(root)
   103→
   104→    latest_goal = find_latest_goal_file(goal_dir)
   105→    goal_version = extract_goal_version(latest_goal)
   106→    has_goal = latest_goal is not None
   107→    has_todo = goal_version is not None and find_latest_todo_file(todo_dir, goal_version) is not None
   108→    has_chat = find_latest_chat_log_file(chat_dir) is not None
   109→    has_agents = agents_path.is_file()
   110→    agents_ready = has_agents and not needs_agents_merge(agents_path)
   111→
   112→    if not memory_dir.exists():
   113→        return "partial" if has_agents or policy_sources else "not-installed"
   114→    if has_goal and has_todo and has_chat and agents_ready:
   115→        return "installed"
   116→    return "partial"
   117→
   118→
   119→def extract_goal_version(path: Path | None) -> str | None:
   120→    if path is None:
   121→        return None
   122→    match = re.fullmatch(r"goal_v(\d{3})_\d{8}\.md", path.name)
   123→    if match is None:
   124→        return None
   125→    return match.group(1)
   126→
   127→
   128→def needs_agents_merge(path: Path) -> bool:
   129→    try:
   130→        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").lower()
   131→    except OSError:
   132→        return True
   133→
   134→    required_checks = [
   135→        has_read_order_signal(text),
   136→        has_memory_layout_signal(text),
   137→        has_active_todo_rule(text),
   138→        has_append_only_chat_log_rule(text),
   139→        has_repo_safety_rule(text),
   140→        has_commit_title_rule(text),
   141→        has_dedicated_memorytree_pr_flow(text),
   142→        has_memorytree_only_scope_rule(text),
   143→        has_auto_merge_rule(text),
   144→    ]
   145→    return not all(required_checks)
   146→
   147→
   148→def matches_any(text: str, patterns: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
   149→    return any(re.search(pattern, text) for pattern in patterns)
   150→
   151→
   152→def matches_all_concepts(text: str, concept_groups: tuple[tuple[str, ...], ...]) -> bool:
   153→    return all(matches_any(text, patterns) for patterns in concept_groups)
   154→
   155→
   156→def has_read_order_signal(text: str) -> bool:
   157→    return matches_any(
   158→        text,
   159→        (
   160→            r"\bread order\b",
   161→            r"\bread .* in order\b",
   162→            r"\u8bfb\u53d6\u987a\u5e8f",
   163→            r"\u6309\u6b64\u987a\u5e8f\u8bfb\u53d6",
   164→        ),
   165→    )
   166→
   167→
   168→def has_memory_layout_signal(text: str) -> bool:
   169→    required_paths = (
   170→        (r"memory/01_goals", r"\b01_goals\b"),
   171→        (r"memory/02_todos", r"\b02_todos\b"),
   172→        (r"memory/03_chat_logs", r"\b03_chat_logs\b"),
   173→    )
   174→    return matches_all_concepts(text, required_paths)
   175→
   176→
   177→def has_active_todo_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   178→    return matches_any(
   179→        text,
   180→        (
   181→            r"keep .*todo .*synchron",
   182→            r"todo .*aligned .*active goal",
   183→            r"(sync|align|keep|maintain|bind).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal",
   184→            r"(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(sync|align|match|bound).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal",
   185→            r"\u5f53\u524d\u5f85\u529e\u5fc5\u987b\u4e0e\u5f53\u524d\u76ee\u6807\u4fdd\u6301\u540c\u6b65",
   186→            r"\u5f85\u529e\u5fc5\u987b\u4e0e\u5f53\u524d\u76ee\u6807\u4fdd\u6301\u540c\u6b65",
   187→        ),
   188→    )
   189→
   190→
   191→def has_append_only_chat_log_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   192→    return matches_any(
   193→        text,
   194→        (
   195→            r"append[- ]only .*chat log",
   196→            r"append[- ]only records",
   197→            r"treat chat logs as append[- ]only",
   198→            r"never rewrite or delete prior entries",
   199→            r"\u5bf9\u8bdd\u65e5\u5fd7\u53ea\u8ffd\u52a0",
   200→            r"\u7981\u6b62\u91cd\u5199\u6216\u5220\u9664\u65e2\u6709\u5185\u5bb9",
   201→        ),
   202→    )
   203→
   204→
   205→def has_repo_safety_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   206→    concept_groups = (
   207→        (r"\bpr\b", r"\bpull request\b", r"\bmerge request\b", r"\u62c9\u53d6\u8bf7\u6c42", r"\u5408\u5e76\u8bf7\u6c42"),
   208→        (r"\bci\b", r"\bchecks?\b", r"\bpipeline\b", r"\be2e\b", r"\u68c0\u67e5", r"\u6d41\u6c34\u7ebf"),
   209→        (r"\breview(?:er|ers)?\b", r"\bapproval(?:s)?\b", r"\u8bc4\u5ba1", r"\u5ba1\u6279"),
   210→        (r"\bbranch(?:es)?\b", r"\bprotected branch(?:es)?\b", r"\u5206\u652f"),
   211→    )
   212→    return matches_all_concepts(text, concept_groups) and matches_any(
   213→        text,
   214→        (
   215→            r"\brepository\b",
   216→            r"\brepo\b",
   217→            r"\bproject\b",
   218→            r"\bhost\b",
   219→            r"\brule(?:s)?\b",
   220→            r"\brequirement(?:s)?\b",
   221→            r"\bpolicy\b",
   222→            r"\bcontrol(?:s)?\b",
   223→            r"\u4ed3\u5e93",
   224→            r"\u89c4\u5219",
   225→            r"\u8981\u6c42",
   226→        ),
   227→    )
   228→
   229→
   230→def has_commit_title_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   231→    return matches_any(
   232→        text,
   233→        (
   234→            r"\bmemorytree\([^)]*\):",
   235→            r"\b[a-z]+\((memorytree)\):",
   236→            r"\bmemorytree:",
   237→            r"memorytree-scoped commit title",
   238→            r"memorytree-specific commit title",
   239→            r"use .*memorytree.*commit title",
   240→            r"\u63d0\u4ea4\u6807\u9898.*memorytree",
   241→        ),
   242→    )
   243→
   244→
   245→def has_dedicated_memorytree_pr_flow(text: str) -> bool:
   246→    concept_groups = (
   247→        (r"\bmemorytree\b", r"\u8bb0\u5fc6"),
   248→        (r"\bbranch(?:es)?\b", r"\u5206\u652f"),
   249→        (r"\bpr\b", r"\bpull request\b", r"\bmerge request\b", r"\u62c9\u53d6\u8bf7\u6c42", r"\u5408\u5e76\u8bf7\u6c42"),
   250→        (r"\bdedicated\b", r"\bseparate\b", r"\bisolated\b", r"\bown\b", r"\u4e13\u7528", r"\u72ec\u7acb"),
   251→    )
   252→    return matches_all_concepts(text, concept_groups)
   253→
   254→
   255→def has_memorytree_only_scope_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   256→    return matches_any(
   257→        text,
   258→        (
   259→            r"memorytree[- ]owned changes",
   260→            r"memorytree[- ]owned files",
   261→            r"memorytree-managed files",
   262→            r"(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree[- ]owned.{0,20}(files|changes)?",
   263→            r"(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)",
   264→            r"only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)",
   265→            r"\u4ec5\u81ea\u52a8\u63d0\u4ea4\u548c\u63a8\u9001.*memorytree.*\u53d8\u66f4",
   266→            r"\u7531 memorytree \u7ba1\u7406",
   267→        ),
   268→    )
   269→
   270→
   271→def has_auto_merge_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   272→    return matches_any(
   273→        text,
   274→        (
   275→            r"auto-merge only when .*permit",
   276→            r"only when repository rules permit",
   277→            r"only when repo rules permit",
   278→            r"only enable auto-merge when.{0,60}(required )?(approvals|checks|reviews?)",
   279→            r"auto-merge.{0,60}(required approvals|required checks|repository rules)",
   280→            r"\u4ec5\u5728\u4ed3\u5e93\u89c4\u5219\u5141\u8bb8\u65f6\u624d\u5f00\u542f\u81ea\u52a8\u5408\u5e76",
   281→        ),
   282→    )
   283→
   284→
   285→def format_result_text(result: dict) -> str:
   286→    lines = [
   287→        f"state_before: {result['state_before']}",
   288→        f"state_after: {result['state_after']}",
   289→        f"effective_locale: {result['effective_locale']}",
   290→        f"agents_action: {result['agents_action']}",
   291→        f"agents_merge_required: {str(result['agents_merge_required']).lower()}",
   292→    ]
   293→    if result["created_dirs"]:
   294→        lines.append("created_dirs:")
   295→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["created_dirs"])
   296→    if result["created_files"]:
   297→        lines.append("created_files:")
   298→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["created_files"])
   299→    if result["preserved_files"]:
   300→        lines.append("preserved_files:")
   301→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["preserved_files"])
   302→    return "\n".join(lines)
   303→
   304→
   305→if __name__ == "__main__":
   306→    raise SystemExit(main())
   307→

    84→    )
    85→    parser.add_argument("--locale", default="auto", help="Requested locale: auto, en, or zh-cn.")
    86→    parser.add_argument("--date", default="", help="Override date as YYYY-MM-DD for deterministic runs.")
    87→    parser.add_argument(
    88→        "--time",
    89→        default="",
    90→        help="Override time as HH:MM. If --date is omitted, use the current local date.",
    91→    )
    92→    parser.add_argument("--format", choices=("text", "json"), default="text", help="Output format.")
    93→    return parser.parse_args()
    94→
    95→
    96→def detect_state(root: Path) -> str:
    97→    memory_dir = root / "Memory"
    98→    goal_dir = root / "Memory" / "01_goals"
    99→    todo_dir = root / "Memory" / "02_todos"
   100→    chat_dir = root / "Memory" / "03_chat_logs"
   101→    agents_path = root / "AGENTS.md"
   102→    policy_sources = find_external_policy_sources(root)
   103→
   104→    latest_goal = find_latest_goal_file(goal_dir)
   105→    goal_version = extract_goal_version(latest_goal)
   106→    has_goal = latest_goal is not None
   107→    has_todo = goal_version is not None and find_latest_todo_file(todo_dir, goal_version) is not None
   108→    has_chat = find_latest_chat_log_file(chat_dir) is not None
   109→    has_agents = agents_path.is_file()
   110→    agents_ready = has_agents and not needs_agents_merge(agents_path)
   111→
   112→    if not memory_dir.exists():
   113→        return "partial" if has_agents or policy_sources else "not-installed"
   114→    if has_goal and has_todo and has_chat and agents_ready:
   115→        return "installed"
   116→    return "partial"
   117→
   118→
   119→def extract_goal_version(path: Path | None) -> str | None:
   120→    if path is None:
   121→        return None
   122→    match = re.fullmatch(r"goal_v(\d{3})_\d{8}\.md", path.name)
   123→    if match is None:
   124→        return None
   125→    return match.group(1)
   126→
   127→
   128→def needs_agents_merge(path: Path) -> bool:
   129→    try:
   130→        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").lower()
   131→    except OSError:
   132→        return True
   133→
   134→    required_checks = [
   135→        has_read_order_signal(text),
   136→        has_memory_layout_signal(text),
   137→        has_active_todo_rule(text),
   138→        has_append_only_chat_log_rule(text),
   139→        has_repo_safety_rule(text),
   140→        has_commit_title_rule(text),
   141→        has_dedicated_memorytree_pr_flow(text),
   142→        has_memorytree_only_scope_rule(text),
   143→        has_auto_merge_rule(text),
   144→    ]
   145→    return not all(required_checks)
   146→
   147→
   148→def matches_any(text: str, patterns: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
   149→    return any(re.search(pattern, text) for pattern in patterns)
   150→
   151→
   152→def matches_all_concepts(text: str, concept_groups: tuple[tuple[str, ...], ...]) -> bool:
   153→    return all(matches_any(text, patterns) for patterns in concept_groups)
   154→
   155→
   156→def has_read_order_signal(text: str) -> bool:
   157→    return matches_any(
   158→        text,
   159→        (
   160→            r"\bread order\b",
   161→            r"\bread .* in order\b",
   162→            r"\u8bfb\u53d6\u987a\u5e8f",
   163→            r"\u6309\u6b64\u987a\u5e8f\u8bfb\u53d6",
   164→        ),
   165→    )
   166→
   167→
   168→def has_memory_layout_signal(text: str) -> bool:
   169→    required_paths = (
   170→        (r"memory/01_goals", r"\b01_goals\b"),
   171→        (r"memory/02_todos", r"\b02_todos\b"),
   172→        (r"memory/03_chat_logs", r"\b03_chat_logs\b"),
   173→    )
   174→    return matches_all_concepts(text, required_paths)
   175→
   176→
   177→def has_active_todo_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   178→    return matches_any(
   179→        text,
   180→        (
   181→            r"keep .*todo .*synchron",
   182→            r"todo .*aligned .*active goal",
   183→            r"(sync|align|keep|maintain|bind).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal",
   184→            r"(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(sync|align|match|bound).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal",
   185→            r"\u5f53\u524d\u5f85\u529e\u5fc5\u987b\u4e0e\u5f53\u524d\u76ee\u6807\u4fdd\u6301\u540c\u6b65",
   186→            r"\u5f85\u529e\u5fc5\u987b\u4e0e\u5f53\u524d\u76ee\u6807\u4fdd\u6301\u540c\u6b65",
   187→        ),
   188→    )
   189→
   190→
   191→def has_append_only_chat_log_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   192→    return matches_any(
   193→        text,
   194→        (
   195→            r"append[- ]only .*chat log",
   196→            r"append[- ]only records",
   197→            r"treat chat logs as append[- ]only",
   198→            r"never rewrite or delete prior entries",
   199→            r"\u5bf9\u8bdd\u65e5\u5fd7\u53ea\u8ffd\u52a0",
   200→            r"\u7981\u6b62\u91cd\u5199\u6216\u5220\u9664\u65e2\u6709\u5185\u5bb9",
   201→        ),
   202→    )
   203→
   204→
   205→def has_repo_safety_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   206→    concept_groups = (
   207→        (r"\bpr\b", r"\bpull request\b", r"\bmerge request\b", r"\u62c9\u53d6\u8bf7\u6c42", r"\u5408\u5e76\u8bf7\u6c42"),
   208→        (r"\bci\b", r"\bchecks?\b", r"\bpipeline\b", r"\be2e\b", r"\u68c0\u67e5", r"\u6d41\u6c34\u7ebf"),
   209→        (r"\breview(?:er|ers)?\b", r"\bapproval(?:s)?\b", r"\u8bc4\u5ba1", r"\u5ba1\u6279"),
   210→        (r"\bbranch(?:es)?\b", r"\bprotected branch(?:es)?\b", r"\u5206\u652f"),
   211→    )
   212→    return matches_all_concepts(text, concept_groups) and matches_any(
   213→        text,
   214→        (
   215→            r"\brepository\b",
   216→            r"\brepo\b",
   217→            r"\bproject\b",
   218→            r"\bhost\b",
   219→            r"\brule(?:s)?\b",
   220→            r"\brequirement(?:s)?\b",
   221→            r"\bpolicy\b",
   222→            r"\bcontrol(?:s)?\b",
   223→            r"\u4ed3\u5e93",
   224→            r"\u89c4\u5219",
   225→            r"\u8981\u6c42",
   226→        ),
   227→    )
   228→
   229→
   230→def has_commit_title_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   231→    return matches_any(
   232→        text,
   233→        (
   234→            r"\bmemorytree\([^)]*\):",
   235→            r"\b[a-z]+\((memorytree)\):",
   236→            r"\bmemorytree:",
   237→            r"memorytree-scoped commit title",
   238→            r"memorytree-specific commit title",
   239→            r"use .*memorytree.*commit title",
   240→            r"\u63d0\u4ea4\u6807\u9898.*memorytree",
   241→        ),
   242→    )
   243→
   244→
   245→def has_dedicated_memorytree_pr_flow(text: str) -> bool:
   246→    concept_groups = (
   247→        (r"\bmemorytree\b", r"\u8bb0\u5fc6"),
   248→        (r"\bbranch(?:es)?\b", r"\u5206\u652f"),
   249→        (r"\bpr\b", r"\bpull request\b", r"\bmerge request\b", r"\u62c9\u53d6\u8bf7\u6c42", r"\u5408\u5e76\u8bf7\u6c42"),
   250→        (r"\bdedicated\b", r"\bseparate\b", r"\bisolated\b", r"\bown\b", r"\u4e13\u7528", r"\u72ec\u7acb"),
   251→    )
   252→    return matches_all_concepts(text, concept_groups)
   253→
   254→
   255→def has_memorytree_only_scope_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   256→    return matches_any(
   257→        text,
   258→        (
   259→            r"memorytree[- ]owned changes",
   260→            r"memorytree[- ]owned files",
   261→            r"memorytree-managed files",
   262→            r"(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree[- ]owned.{0,20}(files|changes)?",
   263→            r"(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)",
   264→            r"only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)",
   265→            r"\u4ec5\u81ea\u52a8\u63d0\u4ea4\u548c\u63a8\u9001.*memorytree.*\u53d8\u66f4",
   266→            r"\u7531 memorytree \u7ba1\u7406",
   267→        ),
   268→    )
   269→
   270→
   271→def has_auto_merge_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   272→    return matches_any(
   273→        text,
   274→        (
   275→            r"auto-merge only when .*permit",
   276→            r"only when repository rules permit",
   277→            r"only when repo rules permit",
   278→            r"only enable auto-merge when.{0,60}(required )?(approvals|checks|reviews?)",
   279→            r"auto-merge.{0,60}(required approvals|required checks|repository rules)",
   280→            r"\u4ec5\u5728\u4ed3\u5e93\u89c4\u5219\u5141\u8bb8\u65f6\u624d\u5f00\u542f\u81ea\u52a8\u5408\u5e76",
   281→        ),
   282→    )
   283→
   284→
   285→def format_result_text(result: dict) -> str:
   286→    lines = [
   287→        f"state_before: {result['state_before']}",
   288→        f"state_after: {result['state_after']}",
   289→        f"effective_locale: {result['effective_locale']}",
   290→        f"agents_action: {result['agents_action']}",
   291→        f"agents_merge_required: {str(result['agents_merge_required']).lower()}",
   292→    ]
   293→    if result["created_dirs"]:
   294→        lines.append("created_dirs:")
   295→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["created_dirs"])
   296→    if result["created_files"]:
   297→        lines.append("created_files:")
   298→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["created_files"])
   299→    if result["preserved_files"]:
   300→        lines.append("preserved_files:")
   301→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["preserved_files"])
   302→    return "\n".join(lines)
   303→
   304→
   305→if __name__ == "__main__":
   306→    raise SystemExit(main())
   307→

Write test files for the Phase 3 project modules in D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow. Create these 3 test files:

1. `tests/project/locale.test.ts`
2. `tests/project/scaffold.test.ts`
3. `tests/project/upgrade.test.ts`

CONSTRAINTS:
- ESM project, vitest globals enabled, import from 'vitest'
- Use `mkdtempSync` for temp dirs, clean up in afterEach with `rmSync`
- Import using `.js` extensions

### locale.test.ts (~15 tests):
Read the source: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\project\locale.ts`

Test functions:
- `normalizeLocale`: 'en'→'en', 'zh-cn'→'zh-cn', 'zh'→'zh-cn', 'en-us'→'en', 'zh-tw'→'zh-cn', 'auto' with root→detects, unknown passes through
- `detectRepoLocale`: English repo (README.md with English text), Chinese repo (README.md with 中文), mixed repo, empty repo→null
- `detectSystemLocale`: mock env vars (LC_ALL=zh_CN→'zh-cn', LANG=en_US→'en', no env→'en')

For detectRepoLocale, create temp dirs with README.md files:
- English: write a README with ~100 English words
- Chinese: write a README with ~50 Chinese characters
- Mixed: write a README with both, balanced

For detectSystemLocale, use `vi.stubEnv()` to set env vars.

### scaffold.test.ts (~20 tests):
Read the source: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\project\scaffold.ts`

Test functions:
- `buildDatetime`: date+time, date only, time only, neither (returns now)
- `createMemoryDirs`: creates 5 dirs, returns created list, idempotent (second call returns empty)
- `resolveScaffoldPaths`: returns correct paths with version defaults (001), finds existing versions
- `findLatestVersion`: with matches, without matches, empty dir
- `findLatestGoalFile`: finds latest by version+date, returns null for empty
- `findLatestTodoFile`: finds matching todo for goal version
- `findLatestChatLogFile`: finds latest by date+time
- `writeTemplate`: writes with placeholder substitution, skips existing (force=false), overwrites (force=true)
- `scaffoldContentFiles`: creates goal+todo+chat files from templates, preserves existing
- `hasPolicyContent`: detects policy text, returns false for non-policy
- `findExternalPolicySources`: finds CODEOWNERS, CONTRIBUTING.md etc.

For template tests, create temp dirs with template files containing `{{KEY}}` placeholders.

### upgrade.test.ts (~15 tests):
Read the source: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\project\upgrade.ts`

Test functions:
- `detectState`: 'not-installed' (empty repo), 'partial' (has AGENTS.md but no Memory/), 'installed' (full setup)
- `extractGoalVersion`: extracts from filename, returns null for non-matching
- `needsAgentsMerge`: returns true for incomplete AGENTS.md, false for complete
- Signal detectors: hasReadOrderSignal, hasMemoryLayoutSignal, hasCommitTitleRule etc. — test each with positive/negative text
- `upgrade`: end-to-end with temp dir (requires template files)
- `formatResultText`: formats output correctly

For the signal detector tests, construct text strings that match/don't match the patterns.

For `upgrade` end-to-end, create a temp dir structure with:
- An assets/templates/en/ directory containing goal.md, todo.md, chat-log.md, agents.md template files with {{PLACEHOLDERS}}

WRITE all 3 test files using the Write tool. Read the source files first.

     2→"""

     4→into MemoryTree using deterministic code-based cleaning.

     7→from __future__ import annotations

     9→import argparse

    21→
    22→
    23→def main() -> int:

    29→    repo_slug = slugify(args.project_name.strip() or root.name, fallback="project")
    30→    global_root = resolve_global_root(args.global_root)

    32→    discovered = discover_source_files(requested_clients)
    33→
    34→    results: list[dict[str, object]] = []
    35→    skipped: list[dict[str, str]] = []

    37→    for client, source in discovered:
    38→        if args.limit and len(results) >= args.limit:
    39→            break
    40→        try:

    42→        except Exception as exc:

    45→                    "client": client,
    46→                    "source": source.as_posix(),
    47→                    "reason": "parse-error",
    48→                    "error": summarize_exception(exc),
    49→                }
    50→            )
    51→            continue
    52→
    53→        candidate_project = infer_project_slug(parsed)
    54→        matches_current_repo = transcript_matches_repo(parsed, root, repo_slug)
    55→        if not transcript_has_content(parsed):
    56→            skipped.append(
    57→                {
    58→                    "client": client,
    59→                    "source": source.as_posix(),
    60→                    "reason": "no-importable-content",
    61→                    "project": candidate_project,
    62→                }
    63→            )
    64→            continue

    66→        if args.scope == "current-project" and not matches_current_repo:

    71→                    "reason": "unrelated-project",
    72→                    "project": candidate_project,
    73→                }
    74→            )
    75→            continue
    76→
    77→        try:
    78→            result = import_transcript(
    79→                parsed=parsed,
    80→                root=root,
    81→                global_root=global_root,
    82→                project_slug=repo_slug if matches_current_repo else candidate_project,
    83→                raw_upload_permission=args.raw_upload_permission if matches_current_repo else "not-applicable",
    84→                mirror_to_repo=matches_current_repo,
    85→            )
    86→        except Exception as exc:
    87→            skipped.append(
    88→                {
    89→                    "client": client,
    90→                    "source": source.as_posix(),
    91→                    "reason": "import-error",
    92→                    "project": candidate_project,
    93→                    "error": summarize_exception(exc),
    94→                }
    95→            )
    96→            continue
    97→        result["matches_current_repo"] = matches_current_repo
    98→        results.append(result)
    99→
   100→    payload = {
   101→        "repo": root.as_posix(),
   102→        "repo_project": repo_slug,
   103→        "scope": args.scope,
   104→        "client_filter": args.client,
   105→        "discovered_count": len(discovered),
   106→        "imported_count": len(results),
   107→        "repo_mirror_count": sum(1 for item in results if item["matches_current_repo"]),
   108→        "global_only_count": sum(1 for item in results if not item["matches_current_repo"]),
   109→        "skipped_count": len(skipped),
   110→        "imports": results,
   111→        "skipped": skipped,
   112→    }
   113→
   114→    if args.format == "json":
   115→        print(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False))
   116→    else:

   121→def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
   122→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Discover and import local AI transcripts into MemoryTree.")
   123→    parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Target repository root.")
   124→    parser.add_argument(
   125→        "--client",
   126→        choices=("all", *sorted(CLIENTS)),
   127→        default="all",
   128→        help="Transcript client family to scan.",
   129→    )
   130→    parser.add_argument(
   131→        "--scope",
   132→        choices=("current-project", "all-projects"),
   133→        default="all-projects",
   134→        help="Import only transcripts related to the current repo, or also backfill other projects into the global archive.",
   135→    )
   136→    parser.add_argument(
   137→        "--project-name",
   138→        default="",
   139→        help="Project label for the current repository. Defaults to the repo folder name.",
   140→    )
   141→    parser.add_argument(
   142→        "--global-root",
   143→        default="",
   144→        help="Override the user-level global transcript root. Defaults to ~/.memorytree/transcripts.",
   145→    )
   146→    parser.add_argument(
   147→        "--raw-upload-permission",
   148→        choices=("not-set", "approved", "denied"),
   149→        default="not-set",
   150→        help="Current repository permission for committing raw transcript files.",
   151→    )
   152→    parser.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=0, help="Optional limit on discovered source files.")
   153→    parser.add_argument("--format", choices=("text", "json"), default="text", help="Output format.")
   154→    return parser.parse_args()
   155→
   156→
   157→def resolve_global_root(path_value: str) -> Path:
   158→    if path_value:
   159→        return Path(path_value).expanduser().resolve()
   160→    return default_global_transcript_root()
   161→
   162→
   163→def format_result_text(payload: dict[str, object]) -> str:
   164→    lines = [
   165→        f"repo: {payload['repo']}",
   166→        f"repo_project: {payload['repo_project']}",
   167→        f"scope: {payload['scope']}",
   168→        f"client_filter: {payload['client_filter']}",
   169→        f"discovered_count: {payload['discovered_count']}",
   170→        f"imported_count: {payload['imported_count']}",
   171→        f"repo_mirror_count: {payload['repo_mirror_count']}",
   172→        f"global_only_count: {payload['global_only_count']}",
   173→        f"skipped_count: {payload['skipped_count']}",
   174→    ]
   175→
   176→    imports = payload["imports"]
   177→    if isinstance(imports, list) and imports:
   178→        lines.append("imports:")
   179→        for item in imports[:10]:
   180→            if isinstance(item, dict):
   181→                lines.append(
   182→                    f"- {item.get('client')} {item.get('project')} {item.get('session_id')} repo_mirror={str(item.get('matches_current_repo')).lower()}"
   183→                )
   184→        if len(imports) > 10:
   185→            lines.append(f"- ... {len(imports) - 10} more")
   186→    return "\n".join(lines)
   187→
   188→
   189→def summarize_exception(exc: Exception) -> str:
   190→    return str(exc).strip() or exc.__class__.__name__
   191→
   192→
   193→if __name__ == "__main__":
   194→    raise SystemExit(main())
   195→

   159→        return Path(path_value).expanduser().resolve()
   160→    return default_global_transcript_root()
   161→
   162→
   163→def format_result_text(payload: dict[str, object]) -> str:
   164→    lines = [
   165→        f"repo: {payload['repo']}",
   166→        f"repo_project: {payload['repo_project']}",
   167→        f"scope: {payload['scope']}",
   168→        f"client_filter: {payload['client_filter']}",
   169→        f"discovered_count: {payload['discovered_count']}",
   170→        f"imported_count: {payload['imported_count']}",
   171→        f"repo_mirror_count: {payload['repo_mirror_count']}",
   172→        f"global_only_count: {payload['global_only_count']}",
   173→        f"skipped_count: {payload['skipped_count']}",
   174→    ]
   175→
   176→    imports = payload["imports"]
   177→    if isinstance(imports, list) and imports:
   178→        lines.append("imports:")
   179→        for item in imports[:10]:
   180→            if isinstance(item, dict):
   181→                lines.append(
   182→                    f"- {item.get('client')} {item.get('project')} {item.get('session_id')} repo_mirror={str(item.get('matches_current_repo')).lower()}"
   183→                )
   184→        if len(imports) > 10:
   185→            lines.append(f"- ... {len(imports) - 10} more")
   186→    return "\n".join(lines)
   187→
   188→
   189→def summarize_exception(exc: Exception) -> str:
   190→    return str(exc).strip() or exc.__class__.__name__
   191→
   192→
   193→if __name__ == "__main__":
   194→    raise SystemExit(main())
   195→

input.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master]

jobs:
  test-typescript:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        node-version: ["20", "22"]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Type check
        run: npm run typecheck

      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Test
        run: npm test

      - name: Verify CLI
        run: node dist/cli.js --version

input.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master]

jobs:
  test-typescript:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        node-version: ["20", "22"]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Type check
        run: npm run typecheck

      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Test
        run: npm test

      - name: Verify CLI
        run: node dist/cli.js --version
